executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 100+ Canadian organizations breached in a single day across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate gangs), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware gang is the most active with 18 victims, followed by Play with 9, and Clop with 14. Critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government have been severely impacted.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 9 Canadian victims including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX, Security ONE Alarm Systems
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 14 Canadian victims including Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, CMH Heli, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, Technikel
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a massive ransomware wave affecting 100+ organizations in a single 24-hour period across all provinces and sectors. Critical sectors hit include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, BioPharma Services, Labtician Ophthalmics, Thunder Bay Counselling, Bison Family Medical), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collège Supérieur De Montréal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware gangs (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross was breached by Sinobi, and Kids & Company was compromised by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware group, Morison Insurance Brokers was breached by Sinobi, and multiple accounting/CPA firms were compromised including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin)

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with a federal deadline of April 30, indicating active exploitation in the wild. The prolific ShinyHunters threat actor has breached Rockstar Games via a supply chain attack, ransoming 80 million records and leaking data after the deadline expired; they also claim breaches of Carnival Corporation (8.7M records) and Amtrak (2.1M emails via Salesforce compromise). Iran-linked IRGC group CyberAv3ngers continues targeting Rockwell PLCs across U.S. critical infrastructure, while Qilin ransomware has posted multiple new victims including The Great Cookie, Henley, and Nanometrics. A critical remote code execution vulnerability has been discovered in the Protobuf.js library used widely in NPM packages.

items.description: Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
items.cia_impact: Not specified in source; CISA KEV listing indicates high severity
items.access_required: Network access assumed based on ActiveMQ architecture

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (80M records), Carnival Corporation (8.7M records), Amtrak (2.1M emails)
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain) for Rockstar attack, T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - ransomware), T1567.002 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), credential theft via Salesforce environment compromise

items.name: CyberAv3ngers (Iran-linked IRGC)
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure, specifically Rockwell PLCs
items.ttps: T1584 (Compromise Infrastructure), T1588 (Obtain Capabilities), targeting industrial control systems and programmable logic controllers in critical infrastructure

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: The Great Cookie, Henley, Nanometrics, NHS vendor (ongoing impact from 2024 attack)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion via leak site publication

items.name: JanelaRAT operators
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1219 (Remote Access Software), banking trojan capabilities for credential theft and financial fraud

items.name: Agent Tesla operators
items.targets: General credential theft and keylogging campaigns
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1056.001 (Input Capture: Keylogging), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol for C2)

canada_landscape.summary: No official CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported. Multiple Canadian generic content posts appeared but contained no cybersecurity threat intelligence relevant to Canadian organizations.
